server.c(3) Library Functions Manual server.c(3)

NAME

server.c - 🌐 Client connection manager: TCP connection, reconnection with exponential backoff, and thread-safe transmission

SYNOPSIS

Macros


#define DEBUG_NETWORK 1
#define DEBUG_THREADS 1
#define DEBUG_MEMORY 1
#define MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY (5 * 1000 * 1000)

Functions


int server_connection_init ()
Initialize the server connection management subsystem. int server_connection_establish (const char *address, int port, int reconnect_attempt, bool first_connection, bool has_ever_connected)
Establish connection to ascii-chat server. bool server_connection_is_active ()
Check if server connection is currently active. socket_t server_connection_get_socket ()
Get current socket file descriptor. acip_transport_t * server_connection_get_transport (void)
Get ACIP transport instance. void server_connection_set_transport (acip_transport_t *transport)
Set ACIP transport instance from connection fallback. uint32_t server_connection_get_client_id ()
Get client ID assigned by server. const char * server_connection_get_ip ()
Get resolved server IP address. void server_connection_set_ip (const char *ip)
Set the server IP address. void server_connection_set_port (int port)
Set the server port. int server_connection_get_port (void)
Get the server port. void server_connection_close ()
Close the server connection gracefully. void server_connection_shutdown ()
Emergency connection shutdown for signal handlers. void server_connection_lost ()
Signal that connection has been lost. bool server_connection_is_lost ()
Check if connection loss has been detected. void server_connection_cleanup ()
Cleanup connection management subsystem. asciichat_error_t threaded_send_packet (packet_type_t type, const void *data, size_t len)
Thread-safe packet transmission. int threaded_send_audio_batch_packet (const float *samples, int num_samples, int batch_count)
Thread-safe batched audio packet transmission. asciichat_error_t threaded_send_audio_opus (const uint8_t *opus_data, size_t opus_size, int sample_rate, int frame_duration)
Thread-safe Opus audio frame transmission. asciichat_error_t threaded_send_audio_opus_batch (const uint8_t *opus_data, size_t opus_size, const uint16_t *frame_sizes, int frame_count)
Thread-safe Opus audio batch packet transmission. asciichat_error_t threaded_send_image_frame (const void *pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height, uint32_t pixel_format)
Thread-safe image frame transmission. asciichat_error_t threaded_send_image_frame_h265 (const void *pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t height)
Send H.265-encoded video frame to server (thread-safe). int threaded_send_ping_packet (void)
Thread-safe ping packet transmission. int threaded_send_pong_packet (void)
Thread-safe pong packet transmission. asciichat_error_t threaded_send_stream_start_packet (uint32_t stream_type)
Thread-safe stream start packet transmission. asciichat_error_t threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect (unsigned short width, unsigned short height)
Thread-safe terminal size packet transmission with auto-detection. int threaded_send_client_join_packet (const char *display_name, uint32_t capabilities)
Thread-safe client join packet transmission.

Variables


crypto_handshake_context_t g_crypto_ctx = {0}
Per-connection crypto handshake context. h265_encoder_t *volatile g_h265_encoder = NULL
Global H.265 encoder context for client video streaming.

Detailed Description

🌐 Client connection manager: TCP connection, reconnection with exponential backoff, and thread-safe transmission

The connection management follows a robust state machine:

1.
Initialization: Socket creation and address resolution
2.
Connection: TCP handshake with configurable timeout
3.
Capability Exchange: Send terminal capabilities and client info
4.
Active Monitoring: Health checks and keepalive management
5.
Disconnection: Graceful or forced connection teardown
6.
Reconnection: Exponential backoff retry logic

Thread Safety

All packet transmission functions use a global send mutex to prevent interleaved packets on the wire. The socket file descriptor is protected with atomic operations for thread-safe access across multiple threads.

Reconnection Strategy

Implements exponential backoff with jitter:

  • Initial delay: 10ms
  • Exponential growth: delay = 10ms + (200ms * attempt)
  • Maximum delay: 5 seconds
  • Jitter: Small random component to prevent thundering herd

Platform Compatibility

Uses platform abstraction layer for:

  • Socket creation and management (Winsock vs BSD sockets)
  • Network error handling and error string conversion
  • Address resolution and connection timeout handling
  • Socket options (keepalive, nodelay)

Integration Points

  • main.c: Calls connection establishment and monitoring functions
  • protocol.c: Uses thread-safe send functions for packet transmission
  • keepalive.c: Monitors connection health and triggers reconnection
  • capture.c: Sends media data through connection
  • audio.c: Sends audio data through connection

Error Handling

Connection errors are classified into:

  • Temporary: Network congestion, temporary DNS failures (retry)
  • Permanent: Invalid address, authentication failure (report and exit)
  • Timeout: Connection establishment timeout (retry with backoff)
  • Loss: Existing connection broken (immediate reconnection attempt)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

2025

Version

2.0

#define MAX_RECONNECT_DELAY (5 * 1000 * 1000)

Maximum delay between reconnection attempts (microseconds)

Function Documentation

void server_connection_cleanup ()

Cleanup connection management subsystem. Closes any active connection and destroys synchronization objects. Called during client shutdown.

Definition at line 1082 of file src/client/server.c.

1082                                  {
1083   if (!GET_OPTION(quiet)) {
1084     log_set_terminal_output(true);
1085   }
1086   server_connection_close();
1087   mutex_destroy(&g_send_mutex);
1088 }




References log_set_terminal_output(),
    mutex_destroy(), and server_connection_close().






void
  server_connection_close ()


Close the server connection gracefully. Close server connection
    gracefully.




Marks connection as inactive and closes the socket. This function
    is safe to call multiple times and from multiple threads.


Definition at line 931 of file
  src/client/server.c.


931                                {
932   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_close() called");
933 
934   // Acquire send mutex to prevent race with threaded_send_image_frame()
935   // This ensures any in-flight sends complete before we destroy the transport
936   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
937   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
938 
939   // Destroy ACIP transport before closing socket
940   if (g_client_transport) {
941     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Destroying transport=%p (is_connected=%s) before closing socket",
942               (void *)g_client_transport, acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport) ? "true" : "false");
943     acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
944     g_client_transport = NULL;
945     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Transport destroyed, g_client_transport set to NULL");
946   }
947 
948   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
949 
950   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
951     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Closing socket: %d", (int)g_sockfd);
952     close_socket(g_sockfd);
953     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
954     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Socket closed");
955   }
956 
957   g_my_client_id = 0;
958 
959   // Cleanup crypto context if encryption was enabled
960   if (g_encryption_enabled) {
961     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Cleaning up crypto context");
962     crypto_handshake_destroy(&g_crypto_ctx);
963     g_encryption_enabled = false;
964   }
965 
966   // Turn ON terminal logging when connection is closed (unless it was disabled with --quiet)
967   if (!GET_OPTION(quiet)) {
968     log_set_terminal_output(true);
969   }
970   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_close() completed");
971 }






References acip_transport_destroy(),
    atomic_store_bool(), close_socket,
    crypto_handshake_destroy(), g_crypto_ctx, and
    log_set_terminal_output().


Referenced by server_connection_cleanup().






int
  server_connection_establish (const char * address, int port, int
  reconnect_attempt, bool first_connection, bool
  has_ever_connected)


Establish connection to ascii-chat server. Attempts to connect to
    the specified server with full capability negotiation. Implements
    reconnection logic with exponential backoff for failed attempts. On
    successful connection, performs initial handshake including terminal
    capabilities and client join protocol.




Parameters


address Server IP address or hostname


port Server port number


reconnect_attempt Current reconnection attempt number (0 for first)


first_connection True if this is the initial connection attempt


has_ever_connected True if a connection was ever successfully
  established


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 353 of file
  src/client/server.c.


354                                                          {
355   (void)first_connection; // Currently unused
356   if (!address || port <= 0) {
357     log_error("Invalid address or port parameters");
358     return -1;
359   }
360 
361   // Close any existing connection
362   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
363     close_socket(g_sockfd);
364     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
365   }
366 
367   // Apply reconnection delay if this is a retry
368   if (reconnect_attempt > 0) {
369     unsigned int delay_us = get_reconnect_delay(reconnect_attempt);
370     // Reconnection attempt logged only to file
371     platform_sleep_us(delay_us);
372 
373     // Check if user requested exit during reconnection delay
374     if (should_exit()) {
375       log_debug("Exit requested during reconnection delay");
376       return -1;
377     }
378   } else {
379     // Initial connection logged only to file
380   }
381 
382   // Check for WebSocket URL - handle separately from TCP
383   if (url_is_websocket(address)) {
384     // WebSocket connection - bypass TCP socket creation
385     // Use the original address (URL already contains port if specified)
386     const char *ws_url = address;
387 
388     // Parse for debug logging
389     url_parts_t url_parts = {0};
390     if (url_parse(address, &url_parts) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
391       log_info("Connecting via WebSocket: %s (scheme=%s, host=%s, port=%d)", ws_url, url_parts.scheme, url_parts.host,
392                url_parts.port);
393 
394       // Set server IP and port for crypto handshake
395       // This ensures the crypto context has proper server address info for known_hosts verification
396       server_connection_set_ip(url_parts.host);
397       log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Set server IP to %s from WebSocket URL", url_parts.host);
398     } else {
399       log_info("Connecting via WebSocket: %s", ws_url);
400     }
401 
402     // Initialize crypto if encryption is enabled
403     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_init()");
404     if (client_crypto_init() != 0) {
405       log_error("Failed to initialize crypto (password required or incorrect)");
406       log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_init() failed");
407       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
408       return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED;
409     }
410 
411     // Get crypto context for transport
412     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_client_is_ready() ? crypto_client_get_context() : NULL;
413 
414     // Create WebSocket transport (handles connection internally)
415     g_client_transport = acip_websocket_client_transport_create("client", ws_url, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
416     if (!g_client_transport) {
417       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket ACIP transport");
418       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
419       return -1;
420     }
421     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Created WebSocket ACIP transport with crypto context");
422 
423     // Set connection as active
424     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, true);
425     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false);
426 
427     // Send initial terminal capabilities to server
428     int result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
429                                                               (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
430     if (result < 0) {
431       log_error("Failed to send initial capabilities to server: %s", network_error_string());
432       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
433       g_client_transport = NULL;
434       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
435       return -1;
436     }
437 
438     // Disable terminal logging after initial setup (for non-snapshot mode)
439     if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && has_ever_connected) {
440       log_set_terminal_output(false);
441     }
442 
443     // Build capabilities flags
444     uint32_t my_capabilities = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO;
445     log_debug("GET_OPTION(audio_enabled) = %d (sending CLIENT_JOIN)", GET_OPTION(audio_enabled));
446     if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
447       log_debug("Adding CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO to capabilities");
448       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO;
449     }
450     if (GET_OPTION(color_mode) != COLOR_MODE_NONE) {
451       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_COLOR;
452     }
453     if (GET_OPTION(stretch)) {
454       my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
455     }
456 
457     // Generate display name from username + PID
458     const char *display_name = platform_get_username();
459     char my_display_name[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
460     int pid = getpid();
461     SAFE_SNPRINTF(my_display_name, sizeof(my_display_name), "%s-%d", display_name, pid);
462     if (threaded_send_client_join_packet(my_display_name, my_capabilities) < 0) {
463       log_error("Failed to send client join packet: %s", network_error_string());
464       acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
465       g_client_transport = NULL;
466       url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
467       return -1;
468     }
469 
470     log_info("WebSocket connection established successfully");
471     url_parts_destroy(&url_parts);
472     return 0;
473   }
474 
475   // Resolve server address using getaddrinfo() for IPv4/IPv6 support
476   // Special handling for localhost: ensure we try both IPv6 (::1) and IPv4 (127.0.0.1)
477   // Many systems only map "localhost" to 127.0.0.1 in /etc/hosts
478   bool is_localhost = (strcmp(address, "localhost") == 0 || is_localhost_ipv4(address) || is_localhost_ipv6(address));
479 
480   struct addrinfo hints, *res = NULL, *addr_iter;
481   memset(&hints, 0, sizeof(hints));
482   hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC; // Allow IPv4 or IPv6
483   hints.ai_socktype = SOCK_STREAM;
484   if (is_localhost) {
485     hints.ai_flags = AI_NUMERICSERV; // Optimize for localhost
486   }
487 
488   char port_str[16];
489   SAFE_SNPRINTF(port_str, sizeof(port_str), "%d", port);
490 
491   // For localhost, try IPv6 loopback (::1) first, then fall back to DNS resolution
492   if (is_localhost) {
493     log_debug("Localhost detected - trying IPv6 loopback [::1]:%s first...", port_str);
494     hints.ai_family = AF_INET6;
495     hints.ai_flags = AI_NUMERICHOST | AI_NUMERICSERV;
496 
497     int ipv6_result = getaddrinfo("::1", port_str, &hints, &res);
498     if (ipv6_result == 0 && res != NULL) {
499       // Try IPv6 loopback connection
500       g_sockfd = socket_create("ipv6", res->ai_family, res->ai_socktype, res->ai_protocol);
501       if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
502         log_debug("Trying IPv6 loopback connection to [::1]:%s...", port_str);
503         if (connect_with_timeout(g_sockfd, res->ai_addr, res->ai_addrlen, CONNECT_TIMEOUT)) {
504           log_debug("Connection successful using IPv6 loopback");
505           SAFE_STRNCPY(g_server_ip, "::1", sizeof(g_server_ip));
506           freeaddrinfo(res);
507           res = NULL; // Prevent double-free at connection_success label
508           goto connection_success;
509         }
510         if (socket_get_error(g_sockfd) != 0) {
511           log_debug("NETWORK_ERROR: %d", (int)socket_get_error(g_sockfd));
512         } else {
513           // log_debug("IPv6 loopback connection failed: %s", network_error_string());
514         }
515         close_socket(g_sockfd);
516         g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
517       }
518       freeaddrinfo(res);
519       res = NULL;
520     }
521 
522     // Check if user requested exit (Ctrl-C) before trying IPv4
523     if (should_exit()) {
524       log_debug("Exit requested during connection attempt");
525       return -1;
526     }
527 
528     // IPv6 failed, try IPv4 loopback (127.0.0.1)
529     log_debug("IPv6 failed, trying IPv4 loopback 127.0.0.1:%s...", port_str);
530     hints.ai_family = AF_INET;
531 
532     int ipv4_result = getaddrinfo("127.0.0.1", port_str, &hints, &res);
533     if (ipv4_result == 0 && res != NULL) {
534       g_sockfd = socket_create("ipv4", res->ai_family, res->ai_socktype, res->ai_protocol);
535       if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
536         log_debug("Trying IPv4 loopback connection to 127.0.0.1:%s...", port_str);
537         if (connect_with_timeout(g_sockfd, res->ai_addr, res->ai_addrlen, CONNECT_TIMEOUT)) {
538           log_debug("Connection successful using IPv4 loopback");
539           SAFE_STRNCPY(g_server_ip, "127.0.0.1", sizeof(g_server_ip));
540           freeaddrinfo(res);
541           res = NULL; // Prevent double-free at connection_success label
542           goto connection_success;
543         }
544         if (socket_get_error(g_sockfd) != 0) {
545           log_debug("NETWORK_ERROR: %d", (int)socket_get_error(g_sockfd));
546         } else {
547           // log_debug("IPv4 loopback connection failed: %s", network_error_string());
548         }
549         close_socket(g_sockfd);
550         g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
551       }
552       freeaddrinfo(res);
553       res = NULL;
554     }
555 
556     // Both IPv6 and IPv4 loopback failed for localhost
557     log_warn("Could not connect to localhost using either IPv6 or IPv4 loopback");
558     return -1;
559   }
560 
561   // For non-localhost addresses, use standard resolution
562   log_debug("Resolving server address '%s' port %s...", address, port_str);
563   hints.ai_family = AF_UNSPEC;
564   hints.ai_flags = 0;
565   int getaddr_result = getaddrinfo(address, port_str, &hints, &res);
566   if (getaddr_result != 0) {
567     log_error("Failed to resolve server address '%s': %s", address, gai_strerror(getaddr_result));
568     return -1;
569   }
570 
571   // Try each address returned by getaddrinfo() until one succeeds
572   // Prefer IPv6 over IPv4: try IPv6 addresses first, then fall back to IPv4
573   for (int address_family = AF_INET6; address_family >= AF_INET; address_family -= (AF_INET6 - AF_INET)) {
574     for (addr_iter = res; addr_iter != NULL; addr_iter = addr_iter->ai_next) {
575       // Skip addresses that don't match current pass (IPv6 first, then IPv4)
576       if (addr_iter->ai_family != address_family) {
577         continue;
578       }
579 
580       // Create socket with appropriate address family
581       const char *socket_name =
582           (addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET6) ? "client_socket_server_ipv6" : "client_socket_server_ipv4";
583       g_sockfd = socket_create(socket_name, addr_iter->ai_family, addr_iter->ai_socktype, addr_iter->ai_protocol);
584       if (g_sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
585         log_debug("Could not create socket for address family %d: %s", addr_iter->ai_family, network_error_string());
586         continue; // Try next address
587       }
588 
589       // Log which address family we're trying
590       if (addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET) {
591         log_debug("Trying IPv4 connection...");
592       } else if (addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET6) {
593         log_debug("Trying IPv6 connection...");
594       }
595 
596       // Attempt connection with timeout
597       if (connect_with_timeout(g_sockfd, addr_iter->ai_addr, addr_iter->ai_addrlen, CONNECT_TIMEOUT)) {
598         // Connection successful!
599         log_debug("Connection successful using %s", addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET    ? "IPv4"
600                                                     : addr_iter->ai_family == AF_INET6 ? "IPv6"
601                                                                                        : "unknown protocol");
602 
603         // Extract server IP address for known_hosts
604         if (format_ip_address(addr_iter->ai_family, addr_iter->ai_addr, g_server_ip, sizeof(g_server_ip)) ==
605             ASCIICHAT_OK) {
606           log_debug("Resolved server IP: %s", g_server_ip);
607         } else {
608           log_warn("Failed to format server IP address");
609         }
610 
611         goto connection_success; // Break out of both loops
612       }
613 
614       // Connection failed - close socket and try next address
615       if (socket_get_error(g_sockfd) != 0) {
616         log_debug("NETWORK_ERROR: %d", (int)socket_get_error(g_sockfd));
617       } else {
618         // log_debug("Connection failed: %s", network_error_string());
619       }
620       close_socket(g_sockfd);
621       g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
622     }
623   }
624 
625 connection_success:
626 
627   if (res) {
628     freeaddrinfo(res);
629   }
630 
631   // If we exhausted all addresses without success, fail
632   if (g_sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
633     log_warn("Could not connect to server %s:%d (tried all addresses)", address, port);
634     return -1;
635   }
636 
637   // Connection successful - extract local port for client ID
638   struct sockaddr_storage local_addr = {0};
639   socklen_t addr_len = sizeof(local_addr);
640   if (getsockname(g_sockfd, (struct sockaddr *)&local_addr, &addr_len) == -1) {
641     log_error("Failed to get local socket address: %s", network_error_string());
642     close_socket(g_sockfd);
643     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
644     return -1;
645   }
646 
647   // Extract port from either IPv4 or IPv6 address
648   int local_port = 0;
649   if (((struct sockaddr *)&local_addr)->sa_family == AF_INET) {
650     local_port = NET_TO_HOST_U16(((struct sockaddr_in *)&local_addr)->sin_port);
651   } else if (((struct sockaddr *)&local_addr)->sa_family == AF_INET6) {
652     local_port = NET_TO_HOST_U16(((struct sockaddr_in6 *)&local_addr)->sin6_port);
653   }
654   g_my_client_id = (uint32_t)local_port;
655 
656   // Mark connection as active immediately after successful socket connection
657   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, true);
658   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false);
659   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_reconnect, false);
660 
661   // Initialize crypto BEFORE starting protocol handshake
662   // Note: server IP is already set above in the connection loop
663   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_init()");
664   if (client_crypto_init() != 0) {
665     log_error("Failed to initialize crypto (password required or incorrect)");
666     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_init() failed");
667     close_socket(g_sockfd);
668     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
669     return CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED; // SSH key password was wrong - no retry
670   }
671 
672   // Perform crypto handshake if encryption is enabled
673   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Calling client_crypto_handshake()");
674   int handshake_result = client_crypto_handshake(g_sockfd);
675   if (handshake_result != 0) {
676     log_error("Crypto handshake failed");
677     log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_handshake() failed with code %d", handshake_result);
678     close_socket(g_sockfd);
679     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
680     FATAL(ERROR_CRYPTO_HANDSHAKE,
681           "Crypto handshake failed with server - this usually indicates a protocol mismatch or network issue");
682   }
683   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: client_crypto_handshake() succeeded");
684 
685   // Create ACIP transport for protocol-agnostic packet sending
686   // The transport wraps the socket with encryption context from the handshake
687   const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_client_is_ready() ? crypto_client_get_context() : NULL;
688 
689   // Create TCP transport (WebSocket is handled earlier in the function)
690   g_client_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("client", g_sockfd, (crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx);
691   if (!g_client_transport) {
692     log_error("Failed to create TCP ACIP transport");
693     close_socket(g_sockfd);
694     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
695     return -1;
696   }
697   log_debug("CLIENT_CONNECT: Created TCP ACIP transport with crypto context");
698 
699   // Turn OFF terminal logging when successfully connected to server
700   // First connection - we'll disable logging after main.c shows the "Connected successfully" message
701   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
702     log_debug("Connected to server - terminal logging will be disabled after initial setup");
703   } else {
704     log_debug("Connected to server - terminal logging kept enabled for snapshot mode");
705   }
706 
707   // Configure socket options for optimal performance
708   if (socket_set_keepalive(g_sockfd, true) < 0) {
709     log_warn("Failed to set socket keepalive: %s", network_error_string());
710   }
711 
712   // Configure socket buffers and TCP_NODELAY for optimal performance
713   asciichat_error_t sock_config_result = socket_configure_buffers(g_sockfd);
714   if (sock_config_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
715     log_warn("Failed to configure socket: %s", network_error_string());
716   }
717 
718   // Send initial terminal capabilities to server (this may generate debug logs)
719   int result = threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect((int)terminal_get_effective_width(),
720                                                             (int)terminal_get_effective_height());
721   if (result < 0) {
722     log_error("Failed to send initial capabilities to server: %s", network_error_string());
723     close_socket(g_sockfd);
724     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
725     return -1;
726   }
727 
728   // Now disable terminal logging after capabilities are sent (for reconnections)
729   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && has_ever_connected) {
730     log_set_terminal_output(false);
731     log_debug("Reconnected to server - terminal logging disabled to prevent interference with ASCII display");
732   }
733 
734   // Send client join packet for multi-user support
735   uint32_t my_capabilities = CLIENT_CAP_VIDEO; // Basic video capability
736   log_debug("GET_OPTION(audio_enabled) = %d (sending CLIENT_JOIN)", GET_OPTION(audio_enabled));
737   if (GET_OPTION(audio_enabled)) {
738     log_debug("Adding CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO to capabilities");
739     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_AUDIO;
740   }
741   if (GET_OPTION(color_mode) != COLOR_MODE_NONE) {
742     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_COLOR;
743   }
744   if (GET_OPTION(stretch)) {
745     my_capabilities |= CLIENT_CAP_STRETCH;
746   }
747 
748   // Generate display name from username + PID
749   const char *display_name = platform_get_username();
750 
751   char my_display_name[MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN];
752   int pid = getpid();
753   SAFE_SNPRINTF(my_display_name, sizeof(my_display_name), "%s-%d", display_name, pid);
754 
755   if (threaded_send_client_join_packet(my_display_name, my_capabilities) < 0) {
756     log_error("Failed to send client join packet: %s", network_error_string());
757     close_socket(g_sockfd);
758     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
759     return -1;
760   }
761 
762   // Connection already marked as active after socket creation
763 
764   return 0;
765 }






References acip_tcp_transport_create(),
    acip_transport_destroy(),
    acip_websocket_client_transport_create(), atomic_store_bool(),
    client_crypto_handshake(), client_crypto_init(),
    close_socket, connect_with_timeout(),
    CONNECTION_ERROR_AUTH_FAILED, crypto_client_get_context(),
    crypto_client_is_ready(), format_ip_address(),
    is_localhost_ipv4(), is_localhost_ipv6(),
    log_set_terminal_output(), network_error_string(),
    platform_sleep_us(), server_connection_set_ip(),
    should_exit(), socket_configure_buffers(),
    terminal_get_effective_height(),
    terminal_get_effective_width(),
    threaded_send_client_join_packet(),
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect(),
    url_is_websocket(), url_parse(), and
    url_parts_destroy().

int
  server_connection_init ()


Initialize the server connection management subsystem. Initialize
    server connection management subsystem.




Sets up the send mutex and initializes connection state variables.
    Must be called once during client startup before any connection
  attempts.




Returns


0 on success, non-zero on failure


Definition at line 309 of file
  src/client/server.c.


309                              {
310   // Initialize mutex for thread-safe packet sending
311   if (mutex_init(&g_send_mutex, "send") != 0) {
312     log_error("Failed to initialize send mutex");
313     return -1;
314   }
315 
316   // Register connection atomics for debug sync state monitoring (one-time registration)
317   static bool connection_atomics_registered = false;
318   if (!connection_atomics_registered) {
319     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_connection_active, "server_connection_active_flag", NULL);
320     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_connection_lost, "server_connection_lost_flag", NULL);
321     NAMED_REGISTER_ATOMIC(&g_should_reconnect, "should_attempt_reconnection_flag", NULL);
322     connection_atomics_registered = true;
323   }
324 
325   // Initialize connection state
326   g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
327   g_client_transport = NULL;
328   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
329   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false);
330   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_reconnect, false);
331   g_my_client_id = 0;
332 
333   return 0;
334 }






References atomic_store_bool(), and
  mutex_init().

Check if server connection is currently active.


Returns


true if connection is active, false otherwise

  src/client/server.c.


774                                    {
775   // For TCP: check socket validity
776   // For WebRTC: socket is INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE but transport exists
777   return atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) && (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE || g_client_transport != NULL);
778 }

void
  server_connection_lost ()


Signal that connection has been lost. Called by other modules
    (typically protocol handlers) when they detect connection failure. Triggers
    reconnection logic in main loop.


Definition at line 1054 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1054                               {
1055   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, true);
1056   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
1057 
1058   // Don't re-enable terminal logging here - let the splash screen handle it
1059   // The reconnection splash will capture and display logs properly
1060   display_full_reset();
1061 }

void
  server_connection_set_transport (acip_transport_t * transport)


Set ACIP transport instance from connection fallback. Used to
    integrate the transport from the 3-stage connection fallback orchestrator
    (TCP → STUN → TURN) into the server connection management
    layer.




Parameters


transport Transport instance created by
  connection_attempt_with_fallback()


Definition at line 812 of file
  src/client/server.c.


812                                                                   {
813   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_set_transport() called with transport=%p", (void *)transport);
814 
815   // Clean up any existing transport
816   if (g_client_transport) {
817     log_warn("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Replacing existing transport=%p with new transport=%p (potential premature "
818              "destruction?)",
819              (void *)g_client_transport, (void *)transport);
820     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] OLD transport is_connected=%s before destruction",
821               acip_transport_is_connected(g_client_transport) ? "true" : "false");
822     acip_transport_destroy(g_client_transport);
823     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] OLD transport destroyed");
824   }
825 
826   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Setting g_client_transport to %p", (void *)transport);
827   g_client_transport = transport;
828 
829   // Mark connection as active when transport is set
830   if (transport) {
831     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Transport is non-NULL, extracting socket...");
832     // Extract socket from transport for backward compatibility with socket-based checks
833     g_sockfd = acip_transport_get_socket(transport);
834     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Socket extracted: %d from transport=%p", (int)g_sockfd, (void *)transport);
835 
836     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, true);
837     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, false); // Reset lost flag for new connection
838     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Server connection transport set and marked active (transport=%p, sockfd=%d, "
839               "is_connected=%s)",
840               (void *)transport, (int)g_sockfd, acip_transport_is_connected(transport) ? "true" : "false");
841   } else {
842     g_sockfd = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
843     atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
844     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] Server connection transport cleared and marked inactive");
845   }
846 
847   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] server_connection_set_transport() completed");
848 }






References acip_transport_destroy(), and
    atomic_store_bool().






void
  server_connection_shutdown ()


Emergency connection shutdown for signal handlers. Emergency
    shutdown for signal handlers.




Performs immediate connection shutdown without waiting for
    graceful close procedures. Uses socket shutdown to interrupt any blocking
    recv() operations in other threads.


Definition at line 1018 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1018                                   {
1019   // NOTE: This function may be called from:
1020   //   - Signal handlers on Unix (async-signal-safe context)
1021   //   - SetConsoleCtrlHandler callback thread on Windows (separate thread context)
1022   // Only use atomic operations and simple system calls - NO mutex locks, NO malloc, NO logging.
1023 
1024   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_active, false);
1025   atomic_store_bool(&g_connection_lost, true);
1026 
1027   if (g_sockfd != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1028     // Only shutdown() the socket to interrupt blocking recv()/send() operations.
1029     // Do NOT close() here - on Windows, closing the socket while another thread
1030     // is using it is undefined behavior and can cause STATUS_STACK_BUFFER_OVERRUN.
1031     // The actual socket close happens in server_connection_close() which is called
1032     // from the main thread after worker threads have been joined.
1033     socket_shutdown(g_sockfd, SHUT_RDWR);
1034   }
1035 
1036   // For WebSocket transport: interrupt blocking recv() calls
1037   // The recv() checks is_connected before waiting for data. Setting this to false
1038   // will cause any blocked recv() to return immediately with CONNECTION_CLOSED.
1039   // We use a function that's async-signal-safe (no mutexes) to do this.
1040   server_connection_interrupt_websocket_recv();
1041 
1042   // DO NOT call log_set_terminal_output() here - it uses mutex which is NOT async-signal-safe.
1043   // The normal cleanup path in shutdown_client() will handle logging state.
1044 }






References atomic_store_bool().


Referenced by client_main(), and
    protocol_stop_connection().






int
  threaded_send_audio_batch_packet (const float * samples, int num_samples, int
  batch_count)


Thread-safe batched audio packet transmission. Sends a batched
    audio packet to the server with proper mutex protection and connection state
    checking. Automatically handles encryption if crypto is ready.




Parameters


samples Audio sample buffer containing batched
  samples


num_samples Total number of samples in the batch


batch_count Number of audio chunks in this batch


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1158 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1158                                                                                              {
1159   // Lock mutex for entire send operation to prevent concurrent socket writes
1160   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1161 
1162   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1163   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1164     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1165     return -1;
1166   }
1167 
1168   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1169   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1170 
1171   // Network I/O happens while holding mutex to prevent concurrent socket writes
1172   asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_audio_batch(transport, samples, (uint32_t)num_samples, (uint32_t)batch_count);
1173 
1174   // Unlock after send completes
1175   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1176 
1177   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1178   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1179     server_connection_lost();
1180     return -1;
1181   }
1182 
1183   return 0;
1184 }






References acip_send_audio_batch(),
    atomic_load_bool(), and server_connection_lost().






asciichat_error_t
  threaded_send_audio_opus (const uint8_t * opus_data, size_t opus_size, int
  sample_rate, int frame_duration)


Thread-safe Opus audio frame transmission. Sends a single
    Opus-encoded audio frame to the server with proper synchronization and
    encryption support.




Parameters


opus_data Opus-encoded audio data


opus_size Size of encoded frame


sample_rate Sample rate in Hz


frame_duration Frame duration in milliseconds


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1200 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1201                                                                {
1202   // Get transport reference while holding mutex (brief lock)
1203   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1204 
1205   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1206   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1207     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1208     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection not active or transport unavailable");
1209   }
1210 
1211   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1212   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1213   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1214 
1215   // Build Opus packet with header (outside mutex - no blocking I/O yet)
1216   size_t header_size = 16; // sample_rate (4), frame_duration (4), reserved (8)
1217   size_t total_size = header_size + opus_size;
1218   void *packet_data = buffer_pool_alloc(NULL, total_size);
1219   if (!packet_data) {
1220     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate buffer for Opus packet: %zu bytes", total_size);
1221   }
1222 
1223   // Write header in network byte order
1224   uint8_t *buf = (uint8_t *)packet_data;
1225   uint32_t sr = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)sample_rate);
1226   uint32_t fd = HOST_TO_NET_U32((uint32_t)frame_duration);
1227   memcpy(buf, &sr, 4);
1228   memcpy(buf + 4, &fd, 4);
1229   memset(buf + 8, 0, 8); // Reserved
1230 
1231   // Copy Opus data
1232   memcpy(buf + header_size, opus_data, opus_size);
1233 
1234   // Network I/O happens OUTSIDE the mutex to prevent deadlock on TCP buffer full
1235   asciichat_error_t result =
1236       packet_send_via_transport(transport, PACKET_TYPE_AUDIO_OPUS_BATCH, packet_data, total_size, 0);
1237 
1238   // Clean up
1239   buffer_pool_free(NULL, packet_data, total_size);
1240 
1241   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1242   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1243     server_connection_lost();
1244     return result;
1245   }
1246 
1247   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1248 }






References atomic_load_bool(), buffer_pool_alloc(),
    buffer_pool_free(), packet_send_via_transport(), and
    server_connection_lost().






asciichat_error_t
  threaded_send_audio_opus_batch (const uint8_t * opus_data, size_t opus_size,
  const uint16_t * frame_sizes, int frame_count)


Thread-safe Opus audio batch packet transmission. Sends a batch of
    Opus-encoded audio frames to the server with proper synchronization and
    encryption support.




Parameters


opus_data Opus-encoded audio data (multiple frames
  concatenated)


opus_size Total size of Opus data in bytes


frame_sizes Array of individual frame sizes (variable-length frames)


frame_count Number of Opus frames in the batch


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1264 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1265                                                                                                {
1266   // Lock mutex for entire send operation to prevent concurrent socket writes
1267   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1268 
1269   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1270   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1271     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1272     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection not active or transport unavailable");
1273   }
1274 
1275   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1276   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1277 
1278   // Network I/O happens while holding mutex to prevent concurrent socket writes
1279   // Opus uses 20ms frames at 48kHz (960 samples = 20ms)
1280   asciichat_error_t result =
1281       acip_send_audio_opus_batch(transport, opus_data, opus_size, frame_sizes, frame_count, 48000, 20);
1282 
1283   // Unlock after send completes
1284   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1285 
1286   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1287   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1288     server_connection_lost();
1289     return result;
1290   }
1291 
1292   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1293 }






References acip_send_audio_opus_batch(),
    atomic_load_bool(), and server_connection_lost().






int
  threaded_send_client_join_packet (const char * display_name, uint32_t
  capabilities)


Thread-safe client join packet transmission. Sends client join
    packet with display name and capabilities to the server.




Parameters


display_name Client display name


capabilities Client capability flags


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1547 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1547                                                                                       {
1548   // Connection and transport availability is checked by threaded_send_packet()
1549 
1550   // Build CLIENT_JOIN packet locally
1551   client_info_packet_t join_packet;
1552   SAFE_MEMSET(&join_packet, sizeof(join_packet), 0, sizeof(join_packet));
1553   join_packet.client_id = HOST_TO_NET_U32(0); // Will be assigned by server
1554   SAFE_SNPRINTF(join_packet.display_name, MAX_DISPLAY_NAME_LEN, "%s", display_name ? display_name : "Unknown");
1555   join_packet.capabilities = HOST_TO_NET_U32(capabilities);
1556 
1557   // Use threaded_send_packet() which handles encryption
1558   int send_result = threaded_send_packet(PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_JOIN, &join_packet, sizeof(join_packet));
1559   if (send_result == 0) {
1560     mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1561     bool active = atomic_load_u64(&g_connection_active);
1562     socket_t socket_snapshot = g_sockfd;
1563     const crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx = crypto_client_is_ready() ? crypto_client_get_context() : NULL;
1564     if (active && socket_snapshot != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
1565       (void)log_network_message(
1566           socket_snapshot, (const struct crypto_context_t *)crypto_ctx, LOG_INFO, REMOTE_LOG_DIRECTION_CLIENT_TO_SERVER,
1567           "CLIENT_JOIN sent (display=\"%s\", capabilities=0x%x)", join_packet.display_name, capabilities);
1568     }
1569     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1570   }
1571   return send_result;
1572 }






References atomic_load_u64(),
    crypto_client_get_context(), crypto_client_is_ready(),
    log_network_message(), and threaded_send_packet().


Referenced by server_connection_establish().






asciichat_error_t
  threaded_send_image_frame (const void * pixel_data, uint32_t width, uint32_t
  height, uint32_t pixel_format)


Thread-safe image frame transmission. Sends image frames with
    serialization via g_send_mutex to prevent race conditions when multiple
    threads write to the same TCP socket.




Parameters


pixel_data Pixel data buffer


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


pixel_format Pixel format (1=RGB24)


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1309 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1310                                                                    {
1311   if (!pixel_data) {
1312     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Pixel data is NULL");
1313   }
1314 
1315   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1316 
1317   // Get transport reference - may be NULL if connection was lost
1318   acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1319   if (!transport || !server_connection_is_active()) {
1320     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1321     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Transport unavailable");
1322   }
1323 
1324   // Send image frame with socket lock held
1325   asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_image_frame(transport, pixel_data, width, height, pixel_format);
1326 
1327   // Unlock after send completes
1328   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1329 
1330   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1331   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1332     server_connection_lost();
1333     return result;
1334   }
1335 
1336   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1337 }






References acip_send_image_frame(),
    server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), and
  server_connection_lost().






asciichat_error_t
  threaded_send_image_frame_h265 (const void * pixel_data, uint32_t width,
  uint32_t height)


Send H.265-encoded video frame to server (thread-safe). Encodes
    RGB frame using H.265 codec before transmission. Much more
    bandwidth-efficient than raw pixel transmission. Uses mutex to prevent
    interleaving with other packet sends.




Parameters


pixel_data RGB pixel data from camera


width Frame width in pixels


height Frame height in pixels


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, error code on failure


Definition at line 1353 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1353                                                                                                           {
1354   if (!pixel_data) {
1355     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Pixel data is NULL");
1356   }
1357 
1358   // Get encoder - may be NULL if not initialized
1359   h265_encoder_t *encoder = g_h265_encoder;
1360   if (!encoder) {
1361     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "H.265 encoder not initialized");
1362   }
1363 
1364   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1365 
1366   // Get transport reference - may be NULL if connection was lost
1367   acip_transport_t *transport = server_connection_get_transport();
1368   if (!transport || !server_connection_is_active()) {
1369     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1370     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Transport unavailable");
1371   }
1372 
1373   // Send H.265 frame with socket lock held
1374   asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_image_frame_h265(transport, encoder, pixel_data, width, height);
1375 
1376   // Unlock after send completes
1377   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1378 
1379   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1380   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1381     server_connection_lost();
1382     return result;
1383   }
1384 
1385   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1386 }






References acip_send_image_frame_h265(),
    g_h265_encoder, server_connection_get_transport(),
    server_connection_is_active(), and
  server_connection_lost().






asciichat_error_t
  threaded_send_packet (packet_type_t type, const void * data, size_t
  len)


Thread-safe packet transmission. Sends a packet to the server with
    proper mutex protection and connection state checking. Automatically handles
    encryption if crypto is ready.




Parameters


type Packet type identifier


data Packet payload


len Payload length


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1111 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1111                                                                                          {
1112   // Lock mutex for entire send operation to prevent concurrent socket writes
1113   mutex_lock(&g_send_mutex);
1114 
1115   // Check connection status and get transport reference
1116   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) || !g_client_transport) {
1117     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() check failed: active=%s, transport=%p",
1118               atomic_load_bool(&g_connection_active) ? "true" : "false", (void *)g_client_transport);
1119     mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1120     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Connection not active or transport unavailable");
1121   }
1122 
1123   // Get transport reference - transport has its own internal synchronization
1124   acip_transport_t *transport = g_client_transport;
1125   log_debug_every(LOG_RATE_SLOW, "[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() using transport=%p, is_connected=%s",
1126                   (void *)transport, acip_transport_is_connected(transport) ? "true" : "false");
1127 
1128   // Network I/O happens while holding mutex to prevent concurrent socket writes
1129   asciichat_error_t result = packet_send_via_transport(transport, type, data, len, 0);
1130 
1131   // Unlock after send completes
1132   mutex_unlock(&g_send_mutex);
1133 
1134   // If send failed due to network error, signal connection loss
1135   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1136     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_LIFECYCLE] threaded_send_packet() send failed, calling server_connection_lost()");
1137     server_connection_lost();
1138     return result;
1139   }
1140 
1141   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
1142 }






References atomic_load_bool(),
    packet_send_via_transport(), and server_connection_lost().


Referenced by threaded_send_client_join_packet(),
    threaded_send_ping_packet(), threaded_send_pong_packet(),
    threaded_send_stream_start_packet(), and
    threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect().

  threaded_send_ping_packet (void )


Thread-safe ping packet transmission.


Returns


0 on success, negative on error

  src/client/server.c.


1395                                     {
1396   // Use threaded_send_packet which handles encryption, mutex locking, and connection state
1397   return threaded_send_packet(PACKET_TYPE_PING, NULL, 0);
1398 }

asciichat_error_t
  threaded_send_stream_start_packet (uint32_t stream_type)


Thread-safe stream start packet transmission.


Parameters


stream_type Type of stream (audio/video)


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1420 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1420                                                                           {
1421   // Connection and transport availability is checked by threaded_send_packet()
1422 
1423   // Build STREAM_START packet locally
1424   uint32_t type_data = HOST_TO_NET_U32(stream_type);
1425 
1426   // Use threaded_send_packet() which handles encryption
1427   return threaded_send_packet(PACKET_TYPE_STREAM_START, &type_data, sizeof(type_data));
1428 }






References threaded_send_packet().


Referenced by protocol_start_connection().






asciichat_error_t
  threaded_send_terminal_size_with_auto_detect (unsigned short width, unsigned
  short height)


Thread-safe terminal size packet transmission with auto-detection.
    Sends terminal capabilities packet to the server including terminal size,
    color capabilities, and rendering preferences. Auto-detects terminal
    capabilities if not explicitly specified.




Parameters


width Terminal width in characters


height Terminal height in characters


Returns


0 on success, negative on error


Definition at line 1443 of file
  src/client/server.c.


1443                                                                                                             {
1444   // Log the dimensions being sent to server (helps debug dimension mismatch issues)
1445   log_debug("Sending terminal size to server: %ux%u (auto_width=%d, auto_height=%d)", width, height,
1446             GET_OPTION(auto_width), GET_OPTION(auto_height));
1447 
1448   // Connection and transport availability is checked by threaded_send_packet()
1449 
1450   // Build terminal capabilities packet locally
1451   // Detect terminal capabilities automatically
1452   terminal_capabilities_t caps = detect_terminal_capabilities();
1453 
1454   // Set wants_padding based on snapshot mode and TTY status
1455   // Disable padding when:
1456   // - In snapshot mode (one frame and exit)
1457   // - When stdout is not a TTY (piped/redirected output)
1458   // Enable padding for interactive terminal sessions
1459   bool is_snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
1460   bool is_interactive = terminal_is_interactive();
1461   caps.wants_padding = is_interactive && !is_snapshot_mode;
1462 
1463   log_debug("Client capabilities: wants_padding=%d (snapshot=%d, interactive=%d, stdin_tty=%d, stdout_tty=%d)",
1464             caps.wants_padding, is_snapshot_mode, is_interactive, terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty());
1465 
1466   // Apply user's color mode override
1467   caps = apply_color_mode_override(caps);
1468 
1469   // Check if detection was reliable, use fallback only for auto-detection
1470   if (!caps.detection_reliable && GET_OPTION(color_mode) == COLOR_MODE_AUTO) {
1471     log_warn("Terminal capability detection not reliable, using fallback");
1472     SAFE_MEMSET(&caps, sizeof(caps), 0, sizeof(caps));
1473     caps.color_level = TERM_COLOR_NONE;
1474     caps.color_count = 2;
1475     caps.capabilities = 0;
1476     SAFE_STRNCPY(caps.term_type, "unknown", sizeof(caps.term_type));
1477     SAFE_STRNCPY(caps.colorterm, "", sizeof(caps.colorterm));
1478     caps.detection_reliable = 0;
1479     // Preserve wants_padding even in fallback mode
1480     caps.wants_padding = is_interactive && !is_snapshot_mode;
1481   }
1482 
1483   // Convert to network packet format with proper byte order
1484   terminal_capabilities_packet_t net_packet;
1485   net_packet.capabilities = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.capabilities);
1486   net_packet.color_level = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.color_level);
1487   net_packet.color_count = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.color_count);
1488   net_packet.render_mode = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.render_mode);
1489   net_packet.width = HOST_TO_NET_U16(width);
1490   net_packet.height = HOST_TO_NET_U16(height);
1491   net_packet.palette_type = HOST_TO_NET_U32(GET_OPTION(palette_type));
1492   net_packet.utf8_support = HOST_TO_NET_U32(caps.utf8_support ? 1 : 0);
1493 
1494   const options_t *opts = options_get();
1495   if (GET_OPTION(palette_type) == PALETTE_CUSTOM && GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set)) {
1496     const char *palette_custom = opts && opts->palette_custom_set ? opts->palette_custom : "";
1497     SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.palette_custom, palette_custom, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom));
1498     net_packet.palette_custom[sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom) - 1] = '\0';
1499   } else {
1500     SAFE_MEMSET(net_packet.palette_custom, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom), 0, sizeof(net_packet.palette_custom));
1501   }
1502 
1503   // Set desired FPS
1504   int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
1505   if (fps > 0) {
1506     net_packet.desired_fps = (uint8_t)(fps > 144 ? 144 : fps);
1507   } else {
1508     net_packet.desired_fps = caps.desired_fps;
1509   }
1510 
1511   if (net_packet.desired_fps == 0) {
1512     net_packet.desired_fps = DEFAULT_MAX_FPS;
1513   }
1514 
1515   SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.term_type, caps.term_type, sizeof(net_packet.term_type));
1516   net_packet.term_type[sizeof(net_packet.term_type) - 1] = '\0';
1517 
1518   SAFE_STRNCPY(net_packet.colorterm, caps.colorterm, sizeof(net_packet.colorterm));
1519   net_packet.colorterm[sizeof(net_packet.colorterm) - 1] = '\0';
1520 
1521   net_packet.detection_reliable = caps.detection_reliable;
1522   // Send UTF-8 support flag: true for AUTO (default) and TRUE settings, false for FALSE setting
1523   net_packet.utf8_support = (GET_OPTION(force_utf8) != UTF8_SETTING_FALSE) ? 1 : 0;
1524 
1525   // Set wants_padding flag (1=padding enabled, 0=no padding for snapshot/piped modes)
1526   net_packet.wants_padding = caps.wants_padding ? 1 : 0;
1527 
1528   // Set codec capabilities (client supports all codecs by default)
1529   net_packet.codec_capabilities_video = HOST_TO_NET_U32(VIDEO_CODEC_CAP_ALL);
1530   net_packet.codec_capabilities_audio = HOST_TO_NET_U32(AUDIO_CODEC_CAP_ALL);
1531 
1532   // Use threaded_send_packet() which handles encryption
1533   return threaded_send_packet(PACKET_TYPE_CLIENT_CAPABILITIES, &net_packet, sizeof(net_packet));
1534 }






References auto_height, auto_width,
    detect_terminal_capabilities(), options_get(),
    terminal_is_interactive(), terminal_is_stdin_tty(),
    terminal_is_stdout_tty(), and threaded_send_packet().


Referenced by protocol_start_connection(), and
    server_connection_establish().








Variable
  Documentation




crypto_handshake_context_t
  g_crypto_ctx = {0}


Per-connection crypto handshake context. Global crypto handshake
    context for this client connection.




Maintains the cryptographic state for the current connection,
    including key exchange state, encryption keys, and handshake progress.




Note


This is not static because it may be accessed from
  crypto.c


Definition at line 211 of file
  src/client/server.c.


211 {0};






Referenced by client_crypto_handshake(),
    client_crypto_init(), crypto_client_cleanup(),
    crypto_client_decrypt_packet(),
    crypto_client_encrypt_packet(), crypto_client_get_context(),
    crypto_client_initiate_rekey(), crypto_client_is_ready(),
    crypto_client_process_rekey_request(),
    crypto_client_process_rekey_response(),
    crypto_client_send_rekey_complete(),
    crypto_client_send_rekey_response(),
    crypto_client_should_rekey(), and
  server_connection_close().






h265_encoder_t* volatile g_h265_encoder = NULL


Global H.265 encoder context for client video streaming. Encodes
    captured RGB frames to H.265/HEVC format before transmission. Initialized in
    client_main() and used by capture thread. Set to NULL during shutdown
    to prevent use-after-free.


Definition at line 220 of file
  src/client/server.c.


Referenced by protocol_stop_connection(), and
    threaded_send_image_frame_h265().
